unit_06 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_06

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 301 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)______ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)______, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)______ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)______, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　neglects; problematic
underappreciatedserveexperimental
underappreciated: standard unit headword | serve: standard unit headword | experimental: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
Many theorists believe that measures to prevent industrial pollution necessarily increase production costs, but several recent reports document innovations that ______ environmental harm while also delivering economic benefits.Many theorists believe that measures to prevent industrial pollution necessarily increase production costs, but several recent reports document innovations that ______ environmental harm while also delivering economic benefits.
答案：D, E　mitigate; curtail
divulgedisclosemitigate
divulge: standard unit headword | disclose: standard unit headword | mitigate: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) about science.Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) about science.Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging
contributenoteworthy
contribute: standard unit headword | noteworthy: standard unit headword
#4 SE
Attempts to calm down the _________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.Attempts to calm down the _________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.
答案：B, F　recalcitrant; restive
impassivebefuddle
impassive: standard unit headword | befuddle: standard unit headword
#5 Text Completion
Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; demurred
notespeculate
note: standard unit headword | speculate: standard unit headword
#6 Text Completion
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
lucrativeeruditeesteem
lucrative: standard unit headword | erudite: standard unit headword | esteem: standard unit headword
#7 Sentence Equivalence
His own writing style was ______: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.His own writing style was ______: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.
答案：B, D　audacious; striking
tartjuxtaposition
tart: standard unit headword | juxtaposition: standard unit headword
#8 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
exoticfeckless
exotic: standard unit headword | feckless: standard unit headword
#9 SE
The space travels described in science fiction stories were always epic adventures, and, when compared with those travels, current journeys in space are much smaller and the tasks performed are far more_________.The space travels described in science fiction stories were always epic adventures, and, when compared with those travels, current journeys in space are much smaller and the tasks performed are far more_________.
答案：B, D　quotidian; prosaic
epicprosaic
epic: standard unit headword | prosaic: standard unit headword

The book captures the ______ of several politicians who spoke publicly of old-time virtues in order to mask private vices.The book captures the ______ of several politicians who spoke publicly of old-time virtues in order to mask private vices.
答案：B, F　dissemblance; duplicity
opportunismprobity
opportunism: standard unit headword | probity: standard unit headword
#11 Text Completion
The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
traumaticriveting
traumatic: standard unit headword | riveting: standard unit headword
#12 Text Completion
The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)______ . These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)______ . These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　self-aggrandizing; value; a product
ambiguousaggrandize
ambiguous: standard unit headword | aggrandize: standard unit headword
#13 Sentence Equivalence
Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often ______: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often ______: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.
答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded
innumerablehearten
innumerable: standard unit headword | hearten: standard unit headword

Transportation maps of Alaska are (i)______ in large part by what they (ii)______: lines identifying roads and railroads. With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)______ of transportation options.Transportation maps of Alaska are (i)______ in large part by what they (ii)______: lines identifying roads and railroads. With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)______ of transportation options.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　characterized; lack; dearth
notableelucidate
notable: standard unit headword | elucidate: standard unit headword
#15 Text Completion
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; accurate; impugned
indictcase
indict: standard unit headword | case: standard unit headword

accommodate

答案：D, E　mendacious; deceitful
statistics

Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword

答案：E　meretricious

bold

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2
答案：A, F　not entirely surprised; discourage

Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
reiterate

Blank 2

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
tilt
tilt: standard unit headword
#52 TC TRIPLE
Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) _________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of \ her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) _________ Was not (iii) _________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) _________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of \ her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) _________ Was not (iii) _________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　noted; approbation; unqualified
note
note: standard unit headword
#53 Sentence Equivalence
The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
recoil
recoil: standard unit headword
#54 Text Completion
Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even ______: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage, and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even ______: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage, and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.
答案：E　inevitable
instantaneous
instantaneous: standard unit headword
#55 Sentence Equivalence
Wilson is wont to emphasize the ______ of ants, how ants with full stomachs will regurgitate liquid food for those without, or how the old will fight so the young can survive.Wilson is wont to emphasize the ______ of ants, how ants with full stomachs will regurgitate liquid food for those without, or how the old will fight so the young can survive.
答案：A, B　beneficence; altruism
fecundity
fecundity: standard unit headword
#56 SE
Although there is clear and direct evidence for the use of domestic horses in the Bronze Age cultures of the second millennium B.C.E., evidence for the use of horses before this period is ____________.Although there is clear and direct evidence for the use of domestic horses in the Bronze Age cultures of the second millennium B.C.E., evidence for the use of horses before this period is ____________.
答案：A, E　equivocal; ambiguous
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword
#57 Text Completion
The identity of hominid remains found in a cave in the Altai Mountains was ______ until Paabo and his colleagues ended the speculation by showing that DNA sequences indicated the bones belonged to Neanderthals.The identity of hominid remains found in a cave in the Altai Mountains was ______ until Paabo and his colleagues ended the speculation by showing that DNA sequences indicated the bones belonged to Neanderthals.
答案：B　conjectural
cave in
cave in: standard unit headword
#58 Sentence Equivalence
The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the ______ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the ______ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.
答案：E, F　approbation; adulation
opprobrium
opprobrium: standard unit headword
#59 TC DOUBLE
The social factors that have (i)________ the impact of large-scale economic transformations on families have been in decline in many places, leaving families more (ii) ________ as the pace of change has increased and the attendant risks have consequently multiplied.The social factors that have (i)________ the impact of large-scale economic transformations on families have been in decline in many places, leaving families more (ii) ________ as the pace of change has increased and the attendant risks have consequently multiplied.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　mitigated; vulnerable

mitigate: standard unit headword

The genius of the scientific method is that it (i)______ the dictum of Aristotle that the goal of science is knowledge of the ultimate cause of things. True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii)______ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.The genius of the scientific method is that it (i)______ the dictum of Aristotle that the goal of science is knowledge of the ultimate cause of things. True science, we now know, advances human knowledge by (ii)______ ultimate causes and focusing instead on the testing of empirical hypotheses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettisons; ignoring
dictum
dictum: standard unit headword

William Perkins, his ______ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.William Perkins, his ______ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.
答案：A, F　fiery; impassioned
genteel
genteel: standard unit headword

Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian

答案：C　fanciful

答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with

The (ii)______ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　quixotic; dearth; so widely diffused

#68 Text Completion
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

susceptible: standard unit headword

Blank 2

The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility

Blank 2

答案：B　augmenting

Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.

Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative

Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________, even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________, even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging

The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. 答案：C　static

But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　license for fiction; fictive; integral

答案：B, F　riddle; conundrum

答案：A　misleading

note: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.A favorite tactic of George Eliot's irresponsibly (i) __________ biographer is to hoist an alluring psychological flag concerning Eliot and then scrupulously take it down again, because the evidence will not serve to (ii) __________. But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　speculative; keep it up; gratuitous
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#106 Sentence Equivalence
The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to ______ us as to liberate us.The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to ______ us as to liberate us.
答案：B, E　shackle; stifle
stifle
stifle: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　duplicative; muddies

case: standard unit headword

Because it has usually been impossible to exotic species once they have become established, it is prudent to minimize the introduction of such species that have a substantial probability of unwanted impactsBecause it has usually been impossible to exotic species once they have become established, it is prudent to minimize the introduction of such species that have a substantial probability of unwanted impacts
答案：C, E　eradicate; extirpate

Although the employees’ union and company management, entering into contract negotiations, both issued statements encouraging ______, acrimony between the two sides continued unabated.Although the employees’ union and company management, entering into contract negotiations, both issued statements encouraging ______, acrimony between the two sides continued unabated.
答案：D, E　civility; comity
civility
civility: standard unit headword
#110 TC SINGLE
Defenders of Szynski's work claim that he has been __________ not for his science, but merely because of his self-aggrandizing writing style.Defenders of Szynski's work claim that he has been __________ not for his science, but merely because of his self-aggrandizing writing style.
答案：A　disparaged
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#111 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#112 Sentence Equivalence
The environmental threat posed by the New Zealand mud snail, an invasive species, is ______ by the snail’s small size, which facilitates its spread across watersheds through human activity.The environmental threat posed by the New Zealand mud snail, an invasive species, is ______ by the snail’s small size, which facilitates its spread across watersheds through human activity.
答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated
mitigateaggravate
mitigate: standard unit headword | aggravate: standard unit headword

Fill all bianks in the way that best completes the text. Mead was certainly (i) _________ to state the case for Arapesh nonviolence and gentleness in absolute terms, for the Mountain Arapesh had an established tradition of warfare. As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) __________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed umaggressive and unwarlike.Fill all bianks in the way that best completes the text. Mead was certainly (i) _________ to state the case for Arapesh nonviolence and gentleness in absolute terms, for the Mountain Arapesh had an established tradition of warfare. As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) __________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed umaggressive and unwarlike.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　mistaken; defensible

case: standard unit headword
#114 SE
Perhaps the scariest thing about weather extremes is their inherent : just because we are almost certain they will become more frequent does not mean we can pinpoint exactly when and where they will occur.Perhaps the scariest thing about weather extremes is their inherent : just because we are almost certain they will become more frequent does not mean we can pinpoint exactly when and where they will occur.
答案：C, E　unpredictability; fickleness

inherent: standard unit headword

The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require

Why doesn't everyone love sherry, they asked, celebrating it as the most (iii)______ wine in the world.For years, wine writers have (i)______ the public’s (ii)______ the manifest charms of sherry. Why doesn't everyone love sherry, they asked, celebrating it as the most (iii)______ wine in the world.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　lamented; indifference to; underappreciated

#117 TC TRIPLE
Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　affords; getting take-out food; former
deplete
deplete: standard unit headword
#118 TC DOUBLE
Conservationists tend to be (i) ____________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) ____________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) ____________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) ____________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian

case: standard unit headword
#119 Text Completion
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
inherent
inherent: standard unit headword

Art historian Olu Oguibe contests Cavell’s assumption of a human inclination to escape subjectivity, noting in the human species a ________ to challenge the veracity of that which professes to be purely objective.Art historian Olu Oguibe contests Cavell’s assumption of a human inclination to escape subjectivity, noting in the human species a ________ to challenge the veracity of that which professes to be purely objective.
答案：E, F　disposition; proclivity

答案：B　elide
elucidate

#122 Text Completion
The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)______ and (ii)______, prove one of two conclusions: their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)______, or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)______ and (ii)______, prove one of two conclusions: their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)______, or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

serve: standard unit headword
#123 TC TRIPLE
Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i)_____________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii)_____________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii)____________ it.Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i)_____________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii)_____________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii)____________ it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　contemporaneity; compelling; embrace

ambiguous: standard unit headword

Considering that experimental treatments that are (i) __________ one mouse population are, nonetheless, ineffectual with other mice, we might reasonably conclude that many rodent studies are not (ii) ___________.Considering that experimental treatments that are (i) __________ one mouse population are, nonetheless, ineffectual with other mice, we might reasonably conclude that many rodent studies are not (ii) ___________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　successful for; methodologically rigorous
experimental

答案：E, F　concision; pithiness

答案：A　encomiums

That's just a drop in the millpond, however, given that there are perhaps 2.5 million dams in the United States and that most of those removed to date have been relatively (iii) ____________ .(i) ___________ dams that have outlived their usefulness--or whose social and economic utility is (ii)____________ by the environmental harm they do---is an idea that is catching on. Over the past 6 years, some 175 dams have been dismantled across the United States, and more than 600 over the past century. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　Demolishing; overshadowed; insignificant

The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring

(i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery

答案：A　experimental

Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

答案：B, D　simplicity; artlessness

prose: standard unit headword
#155 TC DOUBLE
At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　contingency; inevitable
accommodate
accommodate: standard unit headword
#156 SE
This is a book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.This is a book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.
答案：B, E　plucky; courageous
noteworthy
noteworthy: standard unit headword
#157 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
reiterate
reiterate: standard unit headword
#158 Sentence Equivalence
Although the insistence on balancing spending against tax revenues has contributed to the economy’s stagnation, unfortunately, the government does not seem likely to ______ this rigid policy.Although the insistence on balancing spending against tax revenues has contributed to the economy’s stagnation, unfortunately, the government does not seem likely to ______ this rigid policy.
答案：D, F　repudiate; recant
contribute
contribute: standard unit headword
#159 TC DOUBLE
Those who knew the senator well were convinced that in spite of his (i)___________in larger political matters, there was no(ii)_________in his expressions of feeling for his constituents.Those who knew the senator well were convinced that in spite of his (i)___________in larger political matters, there was no(ii)_________in his expressions of feeling for his constituents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　duplicity; dissemblance
in spite of
in spite of: standard unit headword
#160 SE
Some consider the theorem Emmy Noether first published in 1918 to be as important as Einstein’s theory of relativity, since much of today’s vanguard research in physics is Noether's theorem.Some consider the theorem Emmy Noether first published in 1918 to be as important as Einstein’s theory of relativity, since much of today’s vanguard research in physics is Noether's theorem.
答案：B, D　undergirded by; dependent on
elucidate
elucidate: standard unit headword
#161 TC TRIPLE
One of the remarkable things about (i) __________ is that it can seem so (ii) __________: many of the issues about which people (iii) __________ seem, to the nonzealot, to be gray and ambiguous at the core.One of the remarkable things about (i) __________ is that it can seem so (ii) __________: many of the issues about which people (iii) __________ seem, to the nonzealot, to be gray and ambiguous at the core.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　conviction; unreasonable; feel absolute certainty
serveambiguous
serve: standard unit headword | ambiguous: standard unit headword
#162 Sentence Equivalence
Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes ______ evidence.Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes ______ evidence.
答案：C, D　untethered from; divorced from
bold
bold: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　question the adequacy; conducive to
secure
secure: standard unit headword
#164 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
disclose
disclose: standard unit headword
#165 Sentence Equivalence
Jaime Javier Rodriguez notes that popular art forms often conceal a daunting complexity: frequently what appears ______ becomes intricate and challenging when closely examined.Jaime Javier Rodriguez notes that popular art forms often conceal a daunting complexity: frequently what appears ______ becomes intricate and challenging when closely examined.
答案：B, F　canned; formulaic
note
note: standard unit headword
#166 TC DOUBLE
The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i)________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii)________ public health.The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i)________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii)________ public health.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　inadequate; can emerge too late to protect
ambiguous
ambiguous: standard unit headword

This is a __________ book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.This is a __________ book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.
答案：B, E　plucky; courageous
noteworthy
noteworthy: standard unit headword

Given the ______ of solid case studies of environmental degradation, this new case study adds little to our knowledge of the field.Given the ______ of solid case studies of environmental degradation, this new case study adds little to our knowledge of the field.
答案：B, C　plethora; surfeit

case: standard unit headword
#169 TC DOUBLE
The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i)________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii)________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i)________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii)________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　constrain; vitiate
aggrandize
aggrandize: standard unit headword
#170 SE
A new study may explain people's tendency to be __________ about their own futures: people asked to imagine themselves in both positive and negative future scenarios were subsequently much better at recalling the happy scenarios.A new study may explain people's tendency to be __________ about their own futures: people asked to imagine themselves in both positive and negative future scenarios were subsequently much better at recalling the happy scenarios.
答案：B, D　blithe; optimistic
befuddle
befuddle: standard unit headword
#171 Sentence Equivalence
As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
noteworthy
noteworthy: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on

答案：C, D　an unfolding; a living

#175 TC DOUBLE
Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i)_________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii)_________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i)_________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii)_________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to

ambiguous: standard unit headword
#176 Sentence Equivalence
Asserting a need to preserve the ______ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.Asserting a need to preserve the ______ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.
答案：B, F　adaptability; flexibility

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undertook; platitudinous

答案：D, E　disinformation; mendacity

Blank 1
Blank 2

They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i)________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. Blank 2

#186 Text Completion
The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method—namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i)______. The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii)______ their appearance.The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method—namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i)______. The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii)______ their appearance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　undetermined; await

experimental: standard unit headword | reiterate: standard unit headword
#187 Sentence Equivalence
Although in his new book he tends to repeat himself like a ______ uncle, McHughen makes a persuasive case for the safety of tinkering with genes to create new foods.Although in his new book he tends to repeat himself like a ______ uncle, McHughen makes a persuasive case for the safety of tinkering with genes to create new foods.
答案：D, E　garrulous; loquacious
case
case: standard unit headword
#188 Text Completion
Scientific papers often (i)______ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.Scientific papers often (i)______ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account
serve
serve: standard unit headword
#189 Sentence Equivalence
There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is ______ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is ______ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.
答案：D, F　awkward; ungainly
prose
prose: standard unit headword
#190 Text Completion
The space travels described in science fiction stories always used to be epic adventures, in comparison to which current journeys in space seem quite ______.The space travels described in science fiction stories always used to be epic adventures, in comparison to which current journeys in space seem quite ______.
答案：A　mundane
epic
epic: standard unit headword
#191 Sentence Equivalence
In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s ______ prose into crisp lucidity.In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s ______ prose into crisp lucidity.
答案：A, C　wayward; errant
prose
prose: standard unit headword
#192 Text Completion
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
exotic
exotic: standard unit headword
#193 Sentence Equivalence
The researcher noted that microbes, though ______, make up far more of the living protoplasm on earth than all humans, animals and plants combined.The researcher noted that microbes, though ______, make up far more of the living protoplasm on earth than all humans, animals and plants combined.
答案：A, B　diminutive; minuscular

note: standard unit headword

Recently the novelist has (i)______ the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii)______ narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run wild.Recently the novelist has (i)______ the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii)______ narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run wild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　forsaken; conventional
experimental

The book brings together many valuable reports on conservation projects, but with less variety than might have been wished: nearly half the contributors are from the same state, and consequently, the case studies are similarly ______ geographically.The book brings together many valuable reports on conservation projects, but with less variety than might have been wished: nearly half the contributors are from the same state, and consequently, the case studies are similarly ______ geographically.
答案：D, F　circumscribed; restricted

case: standard unit headword

When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______ , the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more considered worthy publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______ , the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more considered worthy publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
tilt
tilt: standard unit headword

Despite her rather ______ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.Despite her rather ______ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.
答案：A, C　unconventional; quirky

答案：D　disingenuous

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　sobering; entreating

答案：A　impediment to

The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

#208 Text Completion
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

答案：D, E　belie; mask

#210 Text Completion
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed

experimental: standard unit headword
#211 Sentence Equivalence
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious

feckless: standard unit headword
#212 Text Completion
Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification

答案：C, D　undermined; impaired

#214 Text Completion
By the end of the 1970s, the postmodern novel had degenerated from a bold attempt to (i)______ the conventions of traditional narrative into a literary style as (ii)______ as any other. There are, it seems, (iii)______ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.By the end of the 1970s, the postmodern novel had degenerated from a bold attempt to (i)______ the conventions of traditional narrative into a literary style as (ii)______ as any other. There are, it seems, (iii)______ number of ways to avoid telling a straightforward story.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　subvert; predictable; a limited

Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to ______ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to ______ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.
答案：A, E　hamstring; impair

#216 Text Completion
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data

答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic

#220 Text Completion
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage

答案：B, E　exalt; valorize

Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence

Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. Blank 2

Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Blank 2

Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)______ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)______ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)______ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)______ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)______ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii) ______ through chemical reactions with other compounds. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unstable; fairly quickly destroyed; ephemeral

#258 Text Completion
Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i)______ , yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii)______ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii)______ —seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i)______ , yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii)______ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii)______ —seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better

#259 Text Completion
As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each

Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. #301 Text Completion
Memoirs are inherently (i)______, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)______: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.Memoirs are inherently (i)______, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)______: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory
noteserve
